title: Getting Started with GitHub
content:
### Creating an Account

Go to [github.com](https://github.com) and sign up. Choose a username, provide your email, and set a strong password. Verify your email address to unlock all features.

### SSH Keys Setup

SSH keys let you authenticate to GitHub without typing your password each time.

```bash
# Generate a new SSH key
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your_email@example.com"

# Start the ssh-agent in the background
eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"

# Add your key to the agent
ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

# Copy the public key
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
```

Go to **Settings → SSH and GPG keys → New SSH key**, paste your public key, and save.

### Personal Access Tokens

For HTTPS access or API usage, create a personal access token (PAT): **Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Tokens (classic)**. Select scopes like `repo` and `workflow`, then copy the token immediately.

### GitHub Desktop

[GitHub Desktop](https://desktop.github.com) provides a visual interface for Git operations. It supports cloning, committing, branching, syncing, and opening pull requests without touching the command line.

quiz.question: What command generates a new SSH key for GitHub?
Where do you add an SSH public key in GitHub?
